pattern: Only 1s and 2s ever pair up, so a single left-to-right sweep that remembers the most recent index of each of the two values can settle every candidate pair the moment the second half of it is seen.
hints: Zeros never participate in a valid pair, so you can ignore them entirely while scanning.
Walk the array once, remembering the most recent index where you saw a 1 and the most recent index where you saw a 2.
Whenever you land on a 1, if a 2 has already appeared, that's a candidate distance; symmetrically, whenever you land on a 2, check the most recent 1. Keep the smallest distance seen this way.

edge_inputs.description: a 2 appears before any 1, and a closer 1 appears after
many candidate pairs, closest one is in the middle

stress.description: random values at the maximum allowed length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    nums = [rng.randint(0, 2) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
